items.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party Community Strong Australia
items.summary: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

items.title: Parents angry as state-run distance education slashes learning materials
items.summary: Families are angry over a decision to phase out "essential" resources for country kids who learn by distance education.

items.title: SA wildlife carers did not expect to be 'ground zero' for Australia's third H5 bird flu case
items.summary: A South Australian wildlife rescue group is working with local authorities after Australia's third confirmed case of H5 bird flu was detected in a migratory bird.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' on temporary exclusion order issued permit to return home
items.summary: Australia's home affairs minister approves the return of a woman who has links to Islamic State and has been in a Syrian refugee camp, saying the government plans to have her under high-level surveillance.

items.title: How behaviour and biology decide the outbreak equation

items.title: Road train fitted with CT scanner screens remote Northern Territory residents for lung cancer

items.title: Sydney MP pushes to bring human composting to Australia as burial costs rise
items.summary: Advocates say human composting offers a sustainable, greener alternative to burials and crematoriums, and can also provide a cheaper and equitable option after death.

items.summary: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.

items.title: Royal Society of Tasmania turning colonial maritime art album into a digital flip book
items.summary: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

items.title: Vanuatu demands France hand back remote Matthew and Hunter Islands in the Pacific
items.summary: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

items.title: Nitrous oxide imports surge as Australian doctors report increasing harm
items.summary: The importation of nitrous oxide has exploded in the last decade. Nangs are increasingly being used by some to chase a high and causing significant damage.

items.title: Australia’s heatwave history offers lessons for sweltering Europe
items.summary: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

items.title: Hanson's 'monoculture' own goal should have been a free kick for Taylor

items.title: How a night in the Swedish forest healed my inner 14-year-old

items.title: Sydney and Melbourne tipped to lead housing price drops as market cools

items.title: NT Labor politician Manuel Brown caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction
items.summary: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

items.title: Award-winning artist Jane Allan accused of copying second artwork

items.summary: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

items.title: Australia records 113-run win over Pakistan to remain unbeaten at Women's T20 World Cup

items.title: Carley Scott wins long-running Federal Court case against space start-up Equatorial Launch Australia
items.summary: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

items.title: Tasmania Devils VFL captain Jye Menzie on growing up in Bridgewater
